questions.question: Which factor mainly helped the East India Company transform from traders to rulers in India?
questions.explanation: The Company used strong armies and political alliances to gain political control.

questions.question: Which event gave the East India Company the right to collect revenue from Bengal, Bihar and Orissa?
questions.explanation: The Grant of Diwani in 1765 gave the Company the right to collect land revenue.

questions.question: Which Mughal emperor granted the Diwani to the East India Company?
questions.explanation: Shah Alam II granted the Diwani to the East India Company after the Battle of Buxar.

questions.question: Which combination of rulers fought against the British in the Battle of Buxar?

options.A: Siraj-ud-Daulah, Mir Jafar, Shah Alam II
options.B: Mir Qasim, Shuja-ud-Daulah, Shah Alam II
options.C: Tipu Sultan, Hyder Ali, Shah Alam II
options.D: Ranjit Singh, Mir Qasim, Shah Alam II

questions.explanation: Mir Qasim, Shuja-ud-Daulah and Shah Alam II fought against the British in the Battle of Buxar.

questions.question: Which British officer played a major role in the victory at the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: Robert Clive led the British forces during the Battle of Plassey in 1757.

questions.question: Which Nawab was defeated in the Battle of Buxar?
questions.explanation: Shuja-ud-Daulah, the Nawab of Awadh, fought against the British in the Battle of Buxar.

questions.question: Which region became the centre of British political power after the Battle of Plassey?
questions.explanation: After Plassey, Bengal became the centre of British political and economic power.

questions.question: Which British policy allowed the annexation of states without a natural heir?
questions.explanation: The Doctrine of Lapse allowed the British to annex states without a natural heir.

questions.question: Which governor-general is associated with the Doctrine of Lapse?
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie introduced the Doctrine of Lapse policy.

questions.question: Which Indian state was annexed under the Doctrine of Lapse after the death of its ruler without a natural heir?
questions.explanation: Jhansi was annexed by the British using the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Which two rulers of Mysore resisted British expansion in South India?
questions.explanation: Hyder Ali and Tipu Sultan fought several wars against the British.

questions.question: Which political strategy required Indian rulers to accept British troops in their territories?
questions.explanation: Under Subsidiary Alliance, rulers had to maintain British troops in their territory.

questions.question: Who introduced the Subsidiary Alliance system?
questions.explanation: Lord Wellesley introduced the Subsidiary Alliance system.

questions.question: Which Indian power in western India fought several wars with the British?
questions.explanation: The Marathas fought a series of wars with the British for control of India.

questions.question: Which battle marked the beginning of British political control in India?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey in 1757 marked the beginning of British political rule.

questions.question: Which war finally ended the power of Tipu Sultan?
questions.explanation: Tipu Sultan was defeated and killed in the Fourth Mysore War in 1799.

questions.question: Which British strategy helped them control Indian rulers indirectly?
questions.explanation: Subsidiary Alliance allowed the British to control Indian states indirectly.

questions.question: Which region provided the East India Company with huge revenue after 1765?
questions.explanation: Bengal became the main source of revenue for the Company.

questions.question: Which event allowed the Company to expand rapidly in northern India?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Buxar strengthened British political authority in northern India.

questions.question: Which administrative system allowed the Company to control both revenue and administration in Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Dual Government system allowed the Company to control revenue while the Nawab handled administration.

questions.question: Which factor weakened Indian rulers and helped the British expand their rule?
questions.explanation: Political rivalries among Indian rulers made it easier for the British to expand.

questions.question: Which Indian ruler is associated with the resistance against British annexation of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai of Jhansi opposed the British annexation under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.question: Which economic policy helped the Company gain large income from Indian agriculture?
questions.explanation: Revenue collection from land was the main income source for the Company.

questions.question: Which war series weakened the Maratha power in India?
questions.explanation: The Anglo-Maratha Wars weakened Maratha power and strengthened British rule.

questions.question: What was the long-term result of the East India Company's territorial expansion?

options.B: Establishment of British colonial rule in India

questions.explanation: The Company's expansion eventually led to British colonial rule in India.
